title: Lepidotheca aurea

Bryozoans are characterized by their unique, filter-feeding colonies that resemble tiny, branching trees. Lepidotheca aurea is part of the phylum Bryozoa, class Phylactolaemata. intro: Lepidotheca aurea, also known as the Golden Coral Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.
